examples.title: Unification Algorithm
examples.description: Unification is the process of finding a substitution that makes two expressions identical.
examples.code:
UNIFY(expr1, expr2)
 IF expr1 and expr2 are constants THEN
 IF expr1 = expr2 THEN return empty substitution
 ELSE return failure
 ELSE IF expr1 is a variable THEN
 IF expr2 is a variable THEN
 IF expr1 = expr2 THEN return empty substitution
 ELSE return failure
 ELSE
 return {expr1/expr2}
 ELSE IF expr2 is a variable THEN
 return {expr2/expr1}
 ELSE IF expr1 and expr2 are functions THEN
 IF expr1.name = expr2.name AND length(expr1.args) = length(expr2.args) THEN
 substitution = empty substitution
 FOR i FROM 1 TO length(expr1.args) DO
 sub = UNIFY(expr1.args[i], expr2.args[i])
 IF sub = failure THEN return failure
 ELSE substitution = COMPOSE(substitution, sub)
 return substitution
 ELSE return failure
examples.explanation: This pseudocode describes the unification algorithm used in predicate logic to find a substitution that makes two expressions identical.
